NKJV.1: Behold what manner of love the Father has bestowed on us, that we should be called children of God! Therefore the world does not know us, because it did not know Him.
NKJV.2: Beloved, now we are children of God; and it has not yet been revealed what we shall be, but we know that when He is revealed, we shall be like Him, for we shall see Him as He is.
NKJV.3: And everyone who has this hope in Him purifies himself, just as He is pure.
NKJV.4: Whoever commits sin also commits lawlessness, and sin is lawlessness.
NKJV.5: And you know that He was manifested to take away our sins, and in Him there is no sin.
NKJV.6: Whoever abides in Him does not sin. Whoever sins has neither seen Him nor known Him.
NKJV.7: Little children, let no one deceive you. He who practices righteousness is righteous, just as He is righteous.
NKJV.8: He who sins is of the devil, for the devil has sinned from the beginning. For this purpose the Son of God was manifested, that He might destroy the works of the devil.
NKJV.9: Whoever has been born of God does not sin, for His seed remains in him; and he cannot sin, because he has been born of God.
NKJV.10: In this the children of God and the children of the devil are manifest: Whoever does not practice righteousness is not of God, nor is he who does not love his brother.
NKJV.11: For this is the message that you heard from the beginning, that we should love one another,
NKJV.12: not as Cain who was of the wicked one and murdered his brother. And why did he murder him? Because his works were evil and his brother’s righteous.
NKJV.13: Do not marvel, my brethren, if the world hates you.
NKJV.14: We know that we have passed from death to life, because we love the brethren. He who does not love his brother abides in death.
NKJV.15: Whoever hates his brother is a murderer, and you know that no murderer has eternal life abiding in him.
NKJV.16: By this we know love, because He laid down His life for us. And we also ought to lay down our lives for the brethren.
NKJV.17: But whoever has this world’s goods, and sees his brother in need, and shuts up his heart from him, how does the love of God abide in him?
NKJV.18: My little children, let us not love in word or in tongue, but in deed and in truth.
NKJV.19: And by this we know that we are of the truth, and shall assure our hearts before Him.
NKJV.20: For if our heart condemns us, God is greater than our heart, and knows all things.
NKJV.21: Beloved, if our heart does not condemn us, we have confidence toward God.
NKJV.22: And whatever we ask we receive from Him, because we keep His commandments and do those things that are pleasing in His sight.
NKJV.23: And this is His commandment: that we should believe on the name of His Son Jesus Christ and love one another, as He gave us commandment.
NKJV.24: Now he who keeps His commandments abides in Him, and He in him. And by this we know that He abides in us, by the Spirit whom He has given us.

ESV.1: See what kind of love the Father has given to us, that we should be called children of God; and so we are. The reason why the world does not know us is that it did not know him.
ESV.2: Beloved, we are God's children now, and what we will be has not yet appeared; but we know that when he appears  we shall be like him, because we shall see him as he is.
ESV.3: And everyone who thus hopes in him purifies himself as he is pure.
ESV.4: Everyone who makes a practice of sinning also practices lawlessness; sin is lawlessness.
ESV.5: You know that he appeared in order to take away sins, and in him there is no sin.
ESV.6: No one who abides in him keeps on sinning; no one who keeps on sinning has either seen him or known him.
ESV.7: Little children, let no one deceive you. Whoever practices righteousness is righteous, as he is righteous.
ESV.8: Whoever makes a practice of sinning is of the devil, for the devil has been sinning from the beginning. The reason the Son of God appeared was to destroy the works of the devil.
ESV.9: No one born of God makes a practice of sinning, for God's  seed abides in him; and he cannot keep on sinning, because he has been born of God.
ESV.10: By this it is evident who are the children of God, and who are the children of the devil: whoever does not practice righteousness is not of God, nor is the one who does not love his brother.
ESV.11: For this is the message that you have heard from the beginning, that we should love one another.
ESV.12: We should not be like Cain, who was of the evil one and murdered his brother. And why did he murder him? Because his own deeds were evil and his brother's righteous.
ESV.13: Do not be surprised, brothers,  that the world hates you.
ESV.14: We know that we have passed out of death into life, because we love the brothers. Whoever does not love abides in death.
ESV.15: Everyone who hates his brother is a murderer, and you know that no murderer has eternal life abiding in him.
ESV.16: By this we know love, that he laid down his life for us, and we ought to lay down our lives for the brothers.
ESV.17: But if anyone has the world's goods and sees his brother in need, yet closes his heart against him, how does God's love abide in him?
ESV.18: Little children, let us not love in word or talk but in deed and in truth.
ESV.19: By this we shall know that we are of the truth and reassure our heart before him;
ESV.20: for whenever our heart condemns us, God is greater than our heart, and he knows everything.
ESV.21: Beloved, if our heart does not condemn us, we have confidence before God;
ESV.22: and whatever we ask we receive from him, because we keep his commandments and do what pleases him.
ESV.23: And this is his commandment, that we believe in the name of his Son Jesus Christ and love one another, just as he has commanded us.
ESV.24: Whoever keeps his commandments abides in God,  and God  in him. And by this we know that he abides in us, by the Spirit whom he has given us.

NASB.1: See how great a love the Father has bestowed on us, that we would be called children of God; and [such] we are. For this reason the world does not know us, because it did not know Him.
NASB.2: Beloved, now we are children of God, and it has not appeared as yet what we will be. We know that when He appears, we will be like Him, because we will see Him just as He is.
NASB.3: And everyone who has this hope [fixed] on Him purifies himself, just as He is pure.
NASB.4: Everyone who practices sin also practices lawlessness; and sin is lawlessness.
NASB.5: You know that He appeared in order to take away sins; and in Him there is no sin.
NASB.6: No one who abides in Him sins; no one who sins has seen Him or knows Him.
NASB.7: Little children, make sure no one deceives you; the one who practices righteousness is righteous, just as He is righteous;
NASB.8: the one who practices sin is of the devil; for the devil has sinned from the beginning. The Son of God appeared for this purpose, to destroy the works of the devil.
NASB.9: No one who is born of God practices sin, because His seed abides in him; and he cannot sin, because he is born of God.
NASB.10: By this the children of God and the children of the devil are obvious: anyone who does not practice righteousness is not of God, nor the one who does not love his brother.
NASB.11: For this is the message which you have heard from the beginning, that we should love one another;
NASB.12: not as Cain, [who] was of the evil one and slew his brother. And for what reason did he slay him? Because his deeds were evil, and his brother's were righteous.
NASB.13: Do not be surprised, brethren, if the world hates you.
NASB.14: We know that we have passed out of death into life, because we love the brethren. He who does not love abides in death.
NASB.15: Everyone who hates his brother is a murderer; and you know that no murderer has eternal life abiding in him.
NASB.16: We know love by this, that He laid down His life for us; and we ought to lay down our lives for the brethren.
NASB.17: But whoever has the world's goods, and sees his brother in need and closes his heart against him, how does the love of God abide in him?
NASB.18: Little children, let us not love with word or with tongue, but in deed and truth.
NASB.19: We will know by this that we are of the truth, and will assure our heart before Him
NASB.20: in whatever our heart condemns us; for God is greater than our heart and knows all things.
NASB.21: Beloved, if our heart does not condemn us, we have confidence before God;
NASB.22: and whatever we ask we receive from Him, because we keep His commandments and do the things that are pleasing in His sight.
NASB.23: This is His commandment, that we believe in the name of His Son Jesus Christ, and love one another, just as He commanded us.
NASB.24: The one who keeps His commandments abides in Him, and He in him. We know by this that He abides in us, by the Spirit whom He has given us.

NLT.1: See how very much our Father loves us, for he calls us his children, and that is what we are! But the people who belong to this world don’t recognize that we are God’s children because they don’t know him.
NLT.2: Dear friends, we are already God’s children, but he has not yet shown us what we will be like when Christ appears. But we do know that we will be like him, for we will see him as he really is.
NLT.3: And all who have this eager expectation will keep themselves pure, just as he is pure.
NLT.4: Everyone who sins is breaking God’s law, for all sin is contrary to the law of God.
NLT.5: And you know that Jesus came to take away our sins, and there is no sin in him.
NLT.6: Anyone who continues to live in him will not sin. But anyone who keeps on sinning does not know him or understand who he is.
NLT.7: Dear children, don’t let anyone deceive you about this: When people do what is right, it shows that they are righteous, even as Christ is righteous.
NLT.8: But when people keep on sinning, it shows that they belong to the devil, who has been sinning since the beginning. But the Son of God came to destroy the works of the devil.
NLT.9: Those who have been born into God’s family do not make a practice of sinning, because God’s life is in them. So they can’t keep on sinning, because they are children of God.
NLT.10: So now we can tell who are children of God and who are children of the devil. Anyone who does not live righteously and does not love other believers does not belong to God.
NLT.11: This is the message you have heard from the beginning: We should love one another.
NLT.12: We must not be like Cain, who belonged to the evil one and killed his brother. And why did he kill him? Because Cain had been doing what was evil, and his brother had been doing what was righteous.
NLT.13: So don’t be surprised, dear brothers and sisters, if the world hates you.
NLT.14: If we love our brothers and sisters who are believers, it proves that we have passed from death to life. But a person who has no love is still dead.
NLT.15: Anyone who hates another brother or sister is really a murderer at heart. And you know that murderers don’t have eternal life within them.
NLT.16: We know what real love is because Jesus gave up his life for us. So we also ought to give up our lives for our brothers and sisters.
NLT.17: If someone has enough money to live well and sees a brother or sister in need but shows no compassion — how can God’s love be in that person?
NLT.18: Dear children, let’s not merely say that we love each other; let us show the truth by our actions.
NLT.19: Our actions will show that we belong to the truth, so we will be confident when we stand before God.
NLT.20: Even if we feel guilty, God is greater than our feelings, and he knows everything.
NLT.21: Dear friends, if we don’t feel guilty, we can come to God with bold confidence.
NLT.22: And we will receive from him whatever we ask because we obey him and do the things that please him.
NLT.23: And this is his commandment: We must believe in the name of his Son, Jesus Christ, and love one another, just as he commanded us.
NLT.24: Those who obey God’s commandments remain in fellowship with him, and he with them. And we know he lives in us because the Spirit he gave us lives in us.

WEB.1: See how great a love the Father has given to us, that we should be called children of God! For this cause the world doesn’t know us, because it didn’t know him.
WEB.2: Beloved, now we are children of God, and it is not yet revealed what we will be. But we know that, when he is revealed, we will be like him; for we will see him just as he is.
WEB.3: Everyone who has this hope set on him purifies himself, even as he is pure.
WEB.4: Everyone who sins also commits lawlessness. Sin is lawlessness.
WEB.5: You know that he was revealed to take away our sins, and in him is no sin.
WEB.6: Whoever remains in him doesn’t sin. Whoever sins hasn’t seen him and doesn’t know him.
WEB.7: Little children, let no one lead you astray. He who does righteousness is righteous, even as he is righteous.
WEB.8: He who sins is of the devil, for the devil has been sinning from the beginning. To this end the Son of God was revealed: that he might destroy the works of the devil.
WEB.9: Whoever is born of God doesn’t commit sin, because his seed remains in him; and he can’t sin, because he is born of God.
WEB.10: In this the children of God are revealed, and the children of the devil. Whoever doesn’t do righteousness is not of God, neither is he who doesn’t love his brother.
WEB.11: For this is the message which you heard from the beginning, that we should love one another;
WEB.12: unlike Cain, who was of the evil one, and killed his brother. Why did he kill him? Because his deeds were evil, and his brother’s righteous.
WEB.13: Don’t be surprised, my brothers, if the world hates you.
WEB.14: We know that we have passed out of death into life, because we love the brothers. He who doesn’t love his brother remains in death.
WEB.15: Whoever hates his brother is a murderer, and you know that no murderer has eternal life remaining in him.
WEB.16: By this we know love, because he laid down his life for us. And we ought to lay down our lives for the brothers.
WEB.17: But whoever has the world’s goods, and sees his brother in need, and closes his heart of compassion against him, how does God’s love remain in him?
WEB.18: My little children, let’s not love in word only, or with the tongue only, but in deed and truth.
WEB.19: And by this we know that we are of the truth, and persuade our hearts before him,
WEB.20: because if our heart condemns us, God is greater than our heart, and knows all things.
WEB.21: Beloved, if our hearts don’t condemn us, we have boldness toward God;
WEB.22: and whatever we ask, we receive from him, because we keep his commandments and do the things that are pleasing in his sight.
WEB.23: This is his commandment, that we should believe in the name of his Son, Jesus Christ, and love one another, even as he commanded.
WEB.24: He who keeps his commandments remains in him, and he in him. By this we know that he remains in us, by the Spirit which he gave us.

CSB17.1: See what great love <sup>[1]</sup> the Father has given us that we should be called God’s children — and we are! The reason the world does not know us is that it didn’t know him.
CSB17.2: Dear friends, we are God’s children now, and what we will be has not yet been revealed. <sup>ⓗ</sup> We know that when he appears, <sup>[2]</sup> we will be like him <sup>ⓘ</sup> because we will see him as he is. <sup>ⓙ</sup>
CSB17.3: And everyone who has this hope <sup>ⓚ</sup> in him purifies himself just as he is pure. <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.4: Everyone who commits sin practices lawlessness; and sin is lawlessness.
CSB17.5: You know that he was revealed so that he might take away sins, <sup>[3]</sup> <sup>ⓜ</sup> and there is no sin in him. <sup>ⓝ</sup>
CSB17.6: Everyone who remains in him <sup>ⓐ</sup> does not sin; <sup>[4]</sup> <sup>ⓑ</sup> everyone who sins <sup>[5]</sup> has not seen him or known him.
CSB17.7: Children, let no one deceive you. The one who does what is right is righteous, just as he is righteous.
CSB17.8: The one who commits <sup>[6]</sup> sin is of the devil, for the devil has sinned from the beginning. The Son of God <sup>ⓒ</sup> was revealed <sup>ⓓ</sup> for this purpose: to destroy the devil’s works.
CSB17.9: Everyone who has been born of God <sup>ⓔ</sup> does not sin, <sup>[7]</sup> because his seed <sup>ⓕ</sup> remains in him; he is not able to sin, <sup>[8]</sup> because he has been born of God.
CSB17.10: This is how God’s children and the devil’s children become obvious. Whoever does not do what is right is not of God, especially the one who does not love his brother or sister. <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.11: For this is the message you have heard from the beginning: We should love one another,
CSB17.12: unlike Cain, <sup>ⓗ</sup> who was of the evil one <sup>ⓘ</sup> and murdered his brother. And why did he murder him? Because his deeds were evil, and his brother’s were righteous.
CSB17.13: Do not be surprised, brothers and sisters, if the world hates <sup>ⓙ</sup> you.
CSB17.14: We know that we have passed from death to life because we love our brothers and sisters. The one who does not love remains in death. <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.15: Everyone who hates his brother or sister <sup>ⓛ</sup> is a murderer, <sup>ⓜ</sup> and you know that no murderer has eternal life residing in him.
CSB17.16: This is how we have come to know love: He laid down his life <sup>ⓝ</sup> for us. <sup>ⓐ</sup> We should also lay down our lives for our brothers and sisters. <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.17: If anyone has this world’s goods <sup>ⓒ</sup> and sees a fellow believer <sup>[9]</sup> in need <sup>ⓓ</sup> but withholds compassion <sup>ⓔ</sup> from him — how does God’s love reside in him? <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.18: Little children, let us not love in word or speech, but in action and in truth. <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.19: This is how we will know that we belong to the truth <sup>ⓗ</sup> and will reassure our hearts before him
CSB17.20: whenever our hearts condemn us; for God is greater than our hearts, and he knows all things. <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.21: Dear friends, if our hearts don’t condemn us, we have confidence before God
CSB17.22: and receive whatever we ask from him because we keep his commands <sup>ⓙ</sup> and do what is pleasing in his sight.
CSB17.23: Now this is his command: that we believe in the name <sup>ⓚ</sup> of his Son Jesus Christ, and love one another as he commanded us.
CSB17.24: The one who keeps his commands remains in him, <sup>ⓛ</sup> and he in him. And the way we know that he remains in us <sup>ⓜ</sup> is from the Spirit <sup>ⓝ</sup> he has given us.

AMP.1: See what an incredible quality of love the Father has shown to us, that we would [be permitted to] be named and called and counted the children of God! And so we are! For this reason the world does not know us, because it did not know Him.
AMP.2: Beloved, we are [even here and] now children of God, and it is not yet made clear what we will be [after His coming]. We know that when He comes and is revealed, we will [as His children] be like Him, because we will see Him just as He is [in all His glory].
AMP.3: And everyone who has this hope [confidently placed] in Him purifies himself, just as He is pure (holy, undefiled, guiltless).
AMP.4: Everyone who practices sin also practices lawlessness; and sin is lawlessness [ignoring God's law by action or neglect or by tolerating wrongdoing—being unrestrained by His commands and His will].
AMP.5: You know that He appeared [in visible form as a man] in order to take away sins; and in Him there is [absolutely] no sin [for He has neither the sin nature nor has He committed sin or acts worthy of blame].
AMP.6: No one who abides in Him [who remains united in fellowship with Him—deliberately, knowingly, and habitually] practices sin. No one who habitually sins has seen Him or known Him.
AMP.7: Little children (believers, dear ones), do not let anyone lead you astray. The one who practices righteousness [the one who strives to live a consistently honorable life—in private as well as in public—and to conform to God's precepts] is righteous, just as He is righteous.
AMP.8: The one who practices sin [separating himself from God, and offending Him by acts of disobedience, indifference, or rebellion] is of the devil [and takes his inner character and moral values from him, not God]; for the devil has sinned and violated God's law from the beginning. The Son of God appeared for this purpose, to destroy the works of the devil.
AMP.9: No one who is born of God [deliberately, knowingly, and habitually] practices sin, because God's seed [His principle of life, the essence of His righteous character] remains [permanently] in him [who is born again—who is reborn from above—spiritually transformed, renewed, and set apart for His purpose]; and he [who is born again] cannot habitually [live a life characterized by] sin, because he is born of God and longs to please Him.
AMP.10: By this the children of God and the children of the devil are clearly identified: anyone who does not practice righteousness [who does not seek God's will in thought, action, and purpose] is not of God, nor is the one who does not [unselfishly] love his [believing] brother.
AMP.11: For this is the message which you [believers] have heard from the beginning [of your relationship with Christ], that we should [unselfishly] love and seek the best for one another;
AMP.12: and not be like Cain, who was of the evil one and murdered his brother [Abel]. And why did he murder him? Because Cain's deeds were evil, and his brother's were righteous.
AMP.13: Do not be surprised, believers, if the world hates you.
AMP.14: We know that we have passed out of death into Life, because we love the brothers and sisters. He who does not love remains in [spiritual] death.
AMP.15: Everyone who hates (works against) his brother [in Christ] is [at heart] a murderer [by God's standards]; and you know that no murderer has eternal life abiding in him.
AMP.16: By this we know [and have come to understand the depth and essence of His precious] love: that He [willingly] laid down His life for us [because He loved us]. And we ought to lay down our lives for the believers.
AMP.17: But whoever has the world's goods (adequate resources), and sees his brother in need, but has no compassion for him, how does the love of God live in him?
AMP.18: Little children (believers, dear ones), let us not love [merely in theory] with word or with tongue [giving lip service to compassion], but in action and in truth [in practice and in sincerity, because practical acts of love are more than words].
AMP.19: By this we will know [without any doubt] that we are of the truth, and will assure our heart and quiet our conscience before Him
AMP.20: whenever our heart convicts us [in guilt]; for God is greater than our heart and He knows all things [nothing is hidden from Him because we are in His hands].
AMP.21: Beloved, if our heart does not convict us [of guilt], we have confidence [complete assurance and boldness] before God;
AMP.22: and we receive from Him whatever we ask because we [carefully and consistently] keep His commandments and do the things that are pleasing in His sight [habitually seeking to follow His plan for us].
AMP.23: This is His commandment, that we believe [with personal faith and confident trust] in the name of His Son Jesus Christ, and [that we unselfishly] love and seek the best for one another, just as He commanded us.
AMP.24: The one who habitually keeps His commandments [obeying His word and following His precepts, abides and] remains in Him, and He in him. By this we know and have the proof that He [really] abides in us, by the Spirit whom He has given us [as a gift].

MSG.1: What marvelous love the Father has extended to us! Just look at it—we're called children of God! That's who we really are. But that's also why the world doesn't recognize us or take us seriously, because it has no idea who he is or what he's up to.
MSG.2: But friends, that's exactly who we are: children of God. And that's only the beginning. Who knows how we'll end up! What we know is that when Christ is openly revealed, we'll see him—and in seeing him, become like him.
MSG.3: All of us who look forward to his Coming stay ready, with the glistening purity of Jesus' life as a model for our own.
MSG.4: All who indulge in a sinful life are dangerously lawless, for sin is a major disruption of God's order.
MSG.5: Surely you know that Christ showed up in order to get rid of sin. There is no sin in him, and sin is not part of his program.
MSG.6: No one who lives deeply in Christ makes a practice of sin. None of those who do practice sin have taken a good look at Christ. They've got him all backwards.
MSG.7: So, my dear children, don't let anyone divert you from the truth. It's the person who acts right who is right, just as we see it lived out in our righteous Messiah.
MSG.8: Those who make a practice of sin are straight from the Devil, the pioneer in the practice of sin. The Son of God entered the scene to abolish the Devil's ways.
MSG.9: People conceived and brought into life by God don't make a practice of sin. How could they? God's seed is deep within them, making them who they are. It's not in the nature of the God-begotten to practice and parade sin.
MSG.10: Here's how you tell the difference between God's children and the Devil's children: The one who won't practice righteous ways isn't from God, nor is the one who won't love brother or sister. A simple test.
MSG.11: For this is the original message we heard: We should love each other.
MSG.12: We must not be like Cain, who joined the Evil One and then killed his brother. And why did he kill him? Because he was deep in the practice of evil, while the acts of his brother were righteous.
MSG.13: So don't be surprised, friends, when the world hates you. This has been going on a long time.
MSG.14: The way we know we've been transferred from death to life is that we love our brothers and sisters. Anyone who doesn't love is as good as dead.
MSG.15: Anyone who hates a brother or sister is a murderer, and you know very well that eternal life and murder don't go together.
MSG.16: This is how we've come to understand and experience love: Christ sacrificed his life for us. This is why we ought to live sacrificially for our fellow believers, and not just be out for ourselves.
MSG.17: If you see some brother or sister in need and have the means to do something about it but turn a cold shoulder and do nothing, what happens to God's love? It disappears. And you made it disappear.
MSG.18: My dear children, let's not just talk about love; let's practice real love.
MSG.19: This is the only way we'll know we're living truly, living in God's reality.
MSG.20: It's also the way to shut down debilitating self-criticism, even when there is something to it. For God is greater than our worried hearts and knows more about us than we do ourselves.
MSG.21: And friends, once that's taken care of and we're no longer accusing or condemning ourselves, we're bold and free before God!
MSG.22: We're able to stretch our hands out and receive what we asked for because we're doing what he said, doing what pleases him.
MSG.23: Again, this is God's command: to believe in his personally named Son, Jesus Christ. He told us to love each other, in line with the original command.
MSG.24: As we keep his commands, we live deeply and surely in him, and he lives in us. And this is how we experience his deep and abiding presence in us: by the Spirit he gave us.

NIV.1: How great is the love the Father has lavished on us, that we should be called children of God! And that is what we are! The reason the world does not know us is that it did not know him.
NIV.2: Dear friends, now we are children of God, and what we will be has not yet been made known. But we know that when he appears, we shall be like him, for we shall see him as he is.
NIV.3: Everyone who has this hope in him purifies himself, just as he is pure.
NIV.4: Everyone who sins breaks the law; in fact, sin is lawlessness.
NIV.5: But you know that he appeared so that he might take away our sins. And in him is no sin.
NIV.6: No one who lives in him keeps on sinning. No one who continues to sin has either seen him or known him.
NIV.7: Dear children, do not let anyone lead you astray. He who does what is right is righteous, just as he is righteous.
NIV.8: He who does what is sinful is of the devil, because the devil has been sinning from the beginning. The reason the Son of God appeared was to destroy the devil’s work.
NIV.9: No one who is born of God will continue to sin, because God’s seed remains in him; he cannot go on sinning, because he has been born of God.
NIV.10: This is how we know who the children of God are and who the children of the devil are: Anyone who does not do what is right is not a child of God; nor is anyone who does not love his brother.
NIV.11:
Love One Another
This is the message you heard from the beginning: We should love one another.
NIV.12: Do not be like Cain, who belonged to the evil one and murdered his brother. And why did he murder him? Because his own actions were evil and his brother’s were righteous.
NIV.13: Do not be surprised, my brothers, if the world hates you.
NIV.14: We know that we have passed from death to life, because we love our brothers. Anyone who does not love remains in death.
NIV.15: Anyone who hates his brother is a murderer, and you know that no murderer has eternal life in him.
NIV.16: This is how we know what love is: Jesus Christ laid down his life for us. And we ought to lay down our lives for our brothers.
NIV.17: If anyone has material possessions and sees his brother in need but has no pity on him, how can the love of God be in him?
NIV.18: Dear children, let us not love with words or tongue but with actions and in truth.
NIV.19: This then is how we know that we belong to the truth, and how we set our hearts at rest in his presence
NIV.20: whenever our hearts condemn us. For God is greater than our hearts, and he knows everything.
NIV.21: Dear friends, if our hearts do not condemn us, we have confidence before God
NIV.22: and receive from him anything we ask, because we obey his commands and do what pleases him.
NIV.23: And this is his command: to believe in the name of his Son, Jesus Christ, and to love one another as he commanded us.
NIV.24: Those who obey his commands live in him, and he in them. And this is how we know that he lives in us: We know it by the Spirit he gave us.

NRSVCE.1: See what love the Father has given us, that we should be called children of God; and that is what we are. The reason the world does not know us is that it did not know him.
NRSVCE.2: Beloved, we are God’s children now; what we will be has not yet been revealed. What we do know is this: when he is revealed, we will be like him, for we will see him as he is.
NRSVCE.3: And all who have this hope in him purify themselves, just as he is pure.
NRSVCE.4: Everyone who commits sin is guilty of lawlessness; sin is lawlessness.
NRSVCE.5: You know that he was revealed to take away sins, and in him there is no sin.
NRSVCE.6: No one who abides in him sins; no one who sins has either seen him or known him.
NRSVCE.7: Little children, let no one deceive you. Everyone who does what is right is righteous, just as he is righteous.
NRSVCE.8: Everyone who commits sin is a child of the devil; for the devil has been sinning from the beginning. The Son of God was revealed for this purpose, to destroy the works of the devil.
NRSVCE.9: Those who have been born of God do not sin, because God’s seed abides in them; they cannot sin, because they have been born of God.
NRSVCE.10: The children of God and the children of the devil are revealed in this way: all who do not do what is right are not from God, nor are those who do not love their brothers and sisters.
NRSVCE.11: For this is the message you have heard from the beginning, that we should love one another.
NRSVCE.12: We must not be like Cain who was from the evil one and murdered his brother. And why did he murder him? Because his own deeds were evil and his brother’s righteous.
NRSVCE.13: Do not be astonished, brothers and sisters, that the world hates you.
NRSVCE.14: We know that we have passed from death to life because we love one another. Whoever does not love abides in death.
NRSVCE.15: All who hate a brother or sister are murderers, and you know that murderers do not have eternal life abiding in them.
NRSVCE.16: We know love by this, that he laid down his life for us — and we ought to lay down our lives for one another.
NRSVCE.17: How does God’s love abide in anyone who has the world’s goods and sees a brother or sister in need and yet refuses help?
NRSVCE.18: Little children, let us love, not in word or speech, but in truth and action.
NRSVCE.19: And by this we will know that we are from the truth and will reassure our hearts before him
NRSVCE.20: whenever our hearts condemn us; for God is greater than our hearts, and he knows everything.
NRSVCE.21: Beloved, if our hearts do not condemn us, we have boldness before God;
NRSVCE.22: and we receive from him whatever we ask, because we obey his commandments and do what pleases him.
NRSVCE.23: And this is his commandment, that we should believe in the name of his Son Jesus Christ and love one another, just as he has commanded us.
NRSVCE.24: All who obey his commandments abide in him, and he abides in them. And by this we know that he abides in us, by the Spirit that he has given us.

NET.1: (See what sort of love the Father has given to us: that we should be called God’s children — and indeed we are! For this reason the world does not know us: because it did not know him.
NET.2: Dear friends, we are God’s children now, and what we will be has not yet been revealed. We know that whenever it is revealed we will be like him, because we will see him just as he is.
NET.3: And everyone who has this hope focused on him purifies himself, just as Jesus is pure).
NET.4: Everyone who practices sin also practices lawlessness; indeed, sin is lawlessness.
NET.5: And you know that Jesus was revealed to take away sins, and in him there is no sin.
NET.6: Everyone who resides in him does not sin; everyone who sins has neither seen him nor known him.
NET.7: Little children, let no one deceive you: The one who practices righteousness is righteous, just as Jesus is righteous.
NET.8: The one who practices sin is of the devil, because the devil has been sinning from the beginning. For this purpose the Son of God was revealed: to destroy the works of the devil.
NET.9: Everyone who has been fathered by God does not practice sin, because God’s seed resides in him, and thus he is not able to sin, because he has been fathered by God.
NET.10: By this the children of God and the children of the devil are revealed: Everyone who does not practice righteousness — the one who does not love his fellow Christian — is not of God.
NET.11: For this is the gospel message that you have heard from the beginning: that we should love one another,
NET.12: not like Cain who was of the evil one and brutally murdered his brother. And why did he murder him? Because his deeds were evil, but his brother’s were righteous.
NET.13: Therefore do not be surprised, brothers and sisters, if the world hates you.
NET.14: We know that we have crossed over from death to life because we love our fellow Christians. The one who does not love remains in death.
NET.15: Everyone who hates his fellow Christian is a murderer, and you know that no murderer has eternal life residing in him.
NET.16: We have come to know love by this: that Jesus laid down his life for us; thus we ought to lay down our lives for our fellow Christians.
NET.17: But whoever has the world’s possessions and sees his fellow Christian in need and shuts off his compassion against him, how can the love of God reside in such a person?
NET.18: Little children, let us not love with word or with tongue but in deed and truth.
NET.19: And by this we will know that we are of the truth and will convince our conscience in his presence,
NET.20: that if our conscience condemns us, that God is greater than our conscience and knows all things.
NET.21: Dear friends, if our conscience does not condemn us, we have confidence in the presence of God,
NET.22: and whatever we ask we receive from him, because we keep his commandments and do the things that are pleasing to him.
NET.23: Now this is his commandment: that we believe in the name of his Son Jesus Christ and love one another, just as he gave us the commandment.
NET.24: And the person who keeps his commandments resides in God, and God in him. Now by this we know that God resides in us: by the Spirit he has given us.
